top_signals.title: The Oil Market Could Be Weeks From a Breaking Point
top_signals.summary: Three and a half months after the blocked Strait of Hormuz created the worst oil supply disruption in history, oil prices remain below $100 per barrel amid hopes of an imminent U.S.-Iran deal. It’s not only hopes that have been keeping prices much lower than a sudden disappearance of 13 million barrels per day (bpd) of supply would warrant. The market has had major buffers to rely on. China, the world’s top crude importer, slashed imports to multi-year lows, while the U.S. boosted its crude exports to a record high. Strategic releases…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: SpaceX 'proxies' plunge as real deal arrives: Here's where traders are buying the dip
top_signals.summary: SpaceX "proxy stocks," whose options volumes were booming ahead of Friday's historic initial public offering, are unwinding gains.

top_signals.title: Challenges and Opportunities of Investing in African Startups
top_signals.summary: Investment into African startups held up in 2025, even as funding conditions tightened. According to AVCA, more than $5 billion was invested in African startups last year, with deal activity also rising. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja spoke with venture capital investor Andrew Firman from Kaleo Ventures on opportunities and challenges of investing in the continent. (Source: Bloomberg)
